qa_pairs.question: What are the consequences if a faculty member with sponsored users fails to renew their Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: If a faculty member with sponsored users allows their Alliance account to expire, both their account and the accounts of all the users they sponsor will be deactivated. This will lead to any jobs submitted by them or their sponsored users being paused, and their research group will lose access to their files until the sponsor account is reactivated.

qa_pairs.question: How does a faculty member renew their Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: To renew their Alliance account, a faculty member will receive an email during the account renewal period with a link to the account renewal form on the CCDB. They must update their contact information and Field of Research, complete a short User Satisfaction Survey, and update the status of their sponsored roles (e.g., students, postdocs). After updating and confirming all information and the status of their sponsored users, they must click the 'REQUEST ACCOUNT RENEWAL' button on the left side of the form to initiate the renewal request.

qa_pairs.question: As a sponsored user, what is the process for renewing my Alliance account, and why might my account still be deactivated after I've completed the renewal steps?
qa_pairs.answer: As a sponsored user, you will receive an email during the account renewal period directing you to the account renewal form. You must update your contact information, complete the form, and fill out a short User Satisfaction Survey. Once all information is updated and confirmed, you need to click the 'REQUEST ACCOUNT RENEWAL' button on the left side of the form to initiate the request. If your account remains deactivated after you have completed these steps, it is because your sponsor has not yet renewed or reactivated their own account. Your role will be activated as soon as your sponsor renews their account and confirms your role.
